level_signals: Expected base pay range $128,000-$160,000 in SF area
Expected to own products end-to-end and communicate to executive levels

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years in product management, product design or equivalent consultant experience with significant time spent leading consumer facing products
You manage project ambiguity, complexity, and interdependencies in an organized and structured way, and you are able to define and analyze metrics that inform the success of your products
Strong user empathy and experience in the customer discovery and product development process

Experience building alignment and influencing decision making across org lines and with senior leadership; specifically showcasing strong judgment when balancing a flawless user experience and the needs of a two sided marketplace
Skills and intuition for communication strategy at every level -- within teams, within the company, to users, and other audiences
Balancing multiple projects, deadlines, and requests should be second nature to you

scope.surface: Driver Earnings — driver-side platform experience, including earnings transparency, fairness, and incentive systems
primary_metrics_inferred: Experiment success metrics tied to driver experience
core_responsibilities: Deeply understand driver and business needs to set an ambitious product vision
Set team goals and success metrics aligned with Lyft's mission
Build a product roadmap and lead a core team of engineers, designers, data scientists, and marketers
Build alignment and collaborate with partner teams on shared goals and initiatives
Communicate roadmaps, priorities, experiments and decisions across a broad set of audiences
Completely own team's products from ideation through development, launch, growth and maintenance

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($128K-$160K base in SF area)
Equity and bonus explicitly mentioned as additional upside
Clear product surface with direct driver impact
Strong benefits package including parental leave, mental health, and 401(k) match
Hybrid schedule with flexibility for up to 4 weeks remote per year
Equal opportunity employer language is specific and inclusive
red_flags: Visa sponsorship status not stated — unclear for international candidates
No nice-to-haves listed, making differentiation criteria opaque
Hybrid role requires in-office presence Mon/Wed/Thu in San Francisco — restricts non-SF candidates
No specific mention of driver earnings domain knowledge or fintech/payments experience, making ideal candidate profile somewhat ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years requirement)
scope.surface (inferred from role title 'Driver Earnings' and JD context)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: No nice-to-haves are explicitly called out in the JD; the experience section appears to be all required, though 'equivalent consultant experience' softens the PM requirement somewhat.
The role title is 'Driver Earnings' but the JD body describes broad driver experience including reducing friction and streamlining interactions — the exact scope boundary is not fully defined.
